     The Numbers Business Report

Happy Feet Two (2011)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $64,006,466Details
International Box Office $93,949,996
Worldwide Box Office $157,956,462
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $20,649,658
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $6,017,304
Total $26,666,962
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Mumble, The Master of Tap, has a problem because his tiny son, Erik, is choreo-phobic. Reluctant to dance, Erik runs away and encounters The Mighty Sven - a penguin who can fly! Mumble has no hope of competing with this charismatic new role model. But things get worse when the world is shaken by powerful forces. Erik learns of his father's guts and grit as Mumble brings together the penguin nations and all manner of fabulous creatures - from tiny Krill to giant Elephant Seals - to put things right.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$21,237,068 (33.2% of total gross)
Legs:3.01 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:40.5% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$135,000,000 (worldwide box office is 1.2 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,606 opening theaters/3,611 max. theaters, 5.1 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $96,517,057

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: November 18th, 2011 (Wide) by Warner Bros.
November 18th, 2011 (IMAX) by Warner Bros., released as Happy Feet 2
International Releases: November 18th, 2011 (Wide) (Nigeria)
December 2nd, 2011 (Wide) (Bulgaria)
December 26th, 2011 (Wide) (New Zealand)
January 5th, 2012 (Wide) (Serbia and Montenegro)
Video Release: March 13th, 2012 by Warner Home Video
MPA Rating: PG for some rude humor and mild peril.
(Rating bulletin 2195, 10/26/2011)
Running Time: 99 minutes
Franchise: Happy Feet
Cast, crew, or production detail: Animal Lead, Motion Capture Performance
Film format: 3-D, 3-D - Shot in 3-D, IMAX: DMR
Plot point: Coming of Age, Dancing, Environment, Talking Animals
Subgenre: Family Musical
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Musical
Production Method:Digital Animation
Creative Type:Kids Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: VR Mumble 2 Productions Pty Ltd.
Production Countries: United States, Australia
Languages: English

Synopsis

Mumble, The Master of Tap, has a problem because his tiny son, Erik, is choreo-phobic. Reluctant to dance, Erik runs away and encounters The Mighty Sven - a penguin who can fly! Mumble has no hope of competing with this charismatic new role model. But things get worse when the world is shaken by powerful forces. Erik learns of his father's guts and grit as Mumble brings together the penguin nations and all manner of fabulous creatures - from tiny Krill to giant Elephant Seals - to put things right.
